title: SDD-089 — Harden matlevering into visible runtime Tiltak ecology

context.setupNotes: Pipeline-plan iteration-2 from a clean worktree branch off origin/main. Source authority: `pm get SDD-089`, AGENTS.md, current code trace, and iteration-2 research artifacts. Existing SDD-089 PM plans and prior plan workspaces were intentionally not used. SDD-089 is architecture-first but not invisible-systems-first: every slice must produce either owner-level proof or visible in-game evidence. Use `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter <name>` for focused verbose GATH filters, then full `./tests/run_tests.sh`. Tests alone do not satisfy courier/held-crate acceptance; record screenshot/video/shot-runner/playthrough trace path or file an explicit tooling follow-up. Review synthesis applied: doorbell sound/presentation check added; director production wiring and handler registration made explicit; raw-string contract set to explicit invalid-record rejection in the director; private/crisis urgent cases and Grete visible intent evidence added; all new tests require red-green doc comments.
designDecisions: `MatleveringSimTick` is a retiring compatibility façade, not the production owner.
`TiltakRuntimeDirector` owns only active-record routing and a tiny once/day ledger; it must not know matlevering domain rules.
Matlevering delivery/doorbell rules live in a narrow delivery handler that emits `DeliveryBus` events and delegates object lifecycle to HOM.
Character urgent response mechanics live behind a character/FSM-owned urgent-activity seam; delivery code must not inspect `current_state` directly.
Runtime Tiltak input is enacted records with lineage/day bounds/parameters, not raw strings; string conversion is limited to retiring façade/test fixtures.
Courier and held crate are acceptance evidence, not polish. Use placeholder visuals if needed, but prove the real visitor path and normal-scene readability.

tasks.title: Task 1: Prove courier visibility through the real visitor path
tasks.description: Slice: NPC visibility. Make a lightweight matlevering courier appear in the normal scene using the existing visitor/NPC presentation path, not a delivery-only debug visual. Keep it small: placeholder courier, entrance placement, no Frank dispatch.
steps: Add a focused failing smoke/behavioral test or scene fixture that spawns a courier through `VisitorManager` and observes `VisitorPresentation`/`VisitorVisual` wiring.
Name the new test/fixture so the planned filter actually matches (for example include `delivery_courier` or `visitor_courier` in the test name).
Verify whether `main.tscn` has a production `VisitorManager`; if missing, wire the smallest manager node under `SimulationRoot` and ensure normal ticking reaches it.
Extend `VisitorVisual.setup()` or equivalent to distinguish a courier placeholder from paramedic visuals without building a full NPC skin system.
Add a helper only if needed, e.g. `spawn_delivery_courier(...)`, but route through `VisitorManager.spawn_visitor()` and `VisitorBus`.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter visitor` or a narrower courier-visible filter.
Temporarily break the visitor bus/presentation hookup to confirm the new test fails red, restore, rerun green, and add `## Verified red-green: YYYY-MM-DD` to the test doc comment.
Capture a normal-scene screenshot/shot-runner/playthrough trace showing the courier at the entrance, or file an explicit PM follow-up if screenshot tooling is missing.
tasks.verification: Focused visitor/courier test passes, and visible evidence proves the courier appears through the real normal-scene visitor path. No debug-only visitor path or Frank dispatch work.

tasks.title: Task 2: Add a minimal held-crate presentation affordance
tasks.description: Slice: held object. Add the smallest presentation-only seam that lets Grete visibly carry a crate. This is not inventory, equipment, or a general item-transfer system.
steps: Write a failing focused test/fixture for a character visual receiving held-object start/end and showing/clearing a crate prop.
Name the new test/fixture so the planned filter actually matches (for example include `held_object` in the test name).
Add a narrow presentation event on `PresentationBus` or an equivalent character-visual API for held-object start/end.
Implement `HeldObjectVisuals` or a small `CharacterVisual` helper using the existing `ActivityVisuals` Sprite3D prop pattern.
Support only the crate prop needed for matlevering, with a clear fallback placeholder.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter held_object` or the matching focused filter.
Temporarily break held-object show/clear to confirm the test fails red, restore, rerun green, and add `## Verified red-green: YYYY-MM-DD` to the test doc comment.
Capture visual evidence or deterministic trace showing Grete/character with the crate prop, then prop cleared.
tasks.verification: Held-object focused test passes; visual evidence/trace shows a readable carried crate; implementation remains presentation-only with no inventory/equipment model.

tasks.title: Task 3: Add enacted Tiltak records and thin `TiltakRuntimeDirector`
tasks.description: Slice: runtime architecture. Establish the production input and routing owner before moving matlevering behavior. The director owns only active-day filtering, handler dispatch, and once/day ledger.
steps: Create `tests/behavioral/tiltak_runtime_director_test.gd` with red coverage for record shape, active-day filtering, once/day duplicate suppression, and explicit invalid-record rejection for raw strings.
Add a small enacted runtime record shape with `id`, `vedtak_id`, `active_from_day`, optional `active_until_day`, and `parameters`.
Add `scripts/simulation/tiltak/tiltak_runtime_director.gd` with handler registration/dispatch and day ledger keyed by record identity + day.
Wire/register the director in the smallest production-facing runtime location used by normal simulation ticks, or add a narrowly named adapter if full day-run integration belongs in Task 7.
Use fake handlers in tests to prove the director passes records through and does not know doorbells, Grete, HOM, or food.

Keep raw string conversion out of the director; if needed, leave conversion only in `MatleveringSimTick` compatibility/test helper. Director tests expect invalid/raw string records to be rejected with an explicit invalid result, not silently ignored.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter tiltak_runtime_director`, mutation-check duplicate suppression/raw-string guard, restore, and rerun green.
tasks.verification: Director tests pass with red-green note. Review confirms the director has no matlevering/doorbell/object/character-state rules and consumes records, not raw strings.

tasks.title: Task 4: Add character-owned urgent activity request seam
tasks.description: Slice: runtime architecture. Move door-answer interrupt/queue mechanics behind the character/FSM boundary so delivery code asks for urgent `Answer Door` instead of reading state and firing FSM events.
steps: Create `tests/behavioral/urgent_activity_request_test.gd` covering idle available Grete, interruptible performing Grete, non-interruptible/private/crisis blocked cases, and the Elling non-responder policy through caller-side role selection.
Add a narrow API such as `CharacterEntity.request_urgent_activity(activity, reason, options := {})` or a small character urgent service.
The seam owns whether to abort, front-queue, kick decision, or reject; outside callers receive accepted/rejected result and reason.
Keep current queue/FSM primitives (`DecisionEngine.queue_activity`, `ActivityExecutor.trigger`) internal to the seam.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter urgent_activity`, mutation-check a blocked case, restore, rerun green.
tasks.verification: Urgent activity tests pass; idle Grete can accept door answer; delivery-domain code has no direct `current_state` checks or direct FSM trigger calls for doorbell behavior.

tasks.title: Task 5: Replace display-name role lookup with stable household identity
tasks.description: Remove Grete/Elling substring matching from production matlevering selection without doing a whole-game identity refactor.
steps: Add the smallest stable identity field needed, likely `character_id` or `household_role` on `resources/scripts/character_profile.gd`, and set it on `resources/characters/mother_grete.tres` and `resources/characters/elling.tres` plus relevant test fixtures.
Add tests proving responder/perceiver lookup survives display-name text changes and does not use `.contains("grete")` / `.contains("elling")`.
Patch matlevering resolver code or the new delivery handler to use stable identity.
Search production matlevering/delivery code for Grete/Elling substring checks and remove them or quarantine them in tests only.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter matlevering` or a narrower role identity filter.
tasks.verification: Stable identity test passes; production delivery/matlevering code no longer selects Grete/Elling by display-name substring. Any broader identity cleanup is filed separately.

tasks.title: Task 6: Guard DD-064 autonomy and preserve outside-delivery retrieval
tasks.description: Fix the broad `requires_tags + advertised_need` autonomy bypass while preserving `Get Delivery From Door` as a narrow delivery affordance.
steps: Add/update a focused test proving a generic TASK with `requires_tags` and `advertised_need` still fails zero-mastery autonomous selection.
Add/update a focused test proving `Get Delivery From Door` remains autonomously eligible only when the outside delivery affordance exists.
Replace the broad `SkillSystem.can_autonomously_select()` bypass with a narrow affordance, preferably data-driven on the activity definition or, if smaller, explicit to `Get Delivery From Door`.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter continuous_mastery` plus the focused delivery/autonomy filter.
Mutation-check by temporarily restoring the broad bypass, confirm red, restore green.
tasks.verification: Generic tagged+advertised TASK remains blocked by DD-064; `Get Delivery From Door` still works through the outside-delivery affordance; red-green note records both sides.

tasks.title: Task 7: Extract `MatleveringDeliveryHandler` and migrate Scenario A/B/C behavior
tasks.description: Move matlevering attempt rules into a narrow delivery handler, preserving SDD-088 behavior while retiring `MatleveringSimTick` as the production owner.
steps: Create `scripts/simulation/delivery/matlevering_delivery_handler.gd` and focused handler tests for previous outside box blocking, answered delivery, unanswered outside box, and HOM calls.

Register the handler with `TiltakRuntimeDirector` explicitly and pass dependencies through a narrow constructor/setup method: HOM access, character resolver, activity definitions, visitor/courier spawner, and delivery bus access.
Wire the director/handler path into the normal production sim tick or day-runtime adapter used by play, not only direct tests.
Do not add a delivery-only courier visual shortcut during handler extraction; courier visibility remains through Task 1 visitor path and Task 8 integration.
Route through `TiltakRuntimeDirector` records and the urgent activity seam; do not inspect character FSM state in the handler.
Use HOM for inside delivered food, outside box, retrieval, replacement, and waste. Handler orchestrates; HOM owns object lifecycle.
Prefer moving Grete-answer inside-food creation to `Answer Door` completion; if a selection-time shortcut remains, document it and guard it with a test named as the chosen contract.

Migrate `matlevering_scenario_a_test.gd` and `matlevering_scenarios_b_c_test.gd` toward director/handler path while preserving behavior: idle Grete answers, Elling alone does not, outside retrieval works, replacement/waste works, previous box blocks.
Keep `MatleveringSimTick` only as a compatibility façade/test adapter if still needed, and quarantine raw-string conversion there.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter matlevering` and `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter tiltak_runtime_director`.
tasks.verification: Scenario A/B/C behavior passes through director/handler/urgent/HOM boundaries. `MatleveringSimTick` is no longer the production owner, raw strings are quarantined, and food timing is explicit/test-backed.
blockedBy: Task 3: Add enacted Tiltak records and thin `TiltakRuntimeDirector`
Task 4: Add character-owned urgent activity request seam
Task 5: Replace display-name role lookup with stable household identity
Task 6: Guard DD-064 autonomy and preserve outside-delivery retrieval

tasks.title: Task 8: Integrate visible matlevering sequence end-to-end
tasks.description: Connect courier, doorbell, urgent Grete response, held crate, HOM kitchen food, and ordinary eating into one normal-play sequence driven by enacted matlevering records.
steps: Add/update an integrated behavioral/smoke test such as `matlevering_visible_sequence_test.gd` using an enacted matlevering record through the director.
When handler attempts delivery, spawn the courier through the visitor path, emit/route `doorbell_rang`, and request urgent `Answer Door` from Grete.
On accepted answer, start held-crate presentation, complete/deposit through HOM, clear crate, and emit the relevant `DeliveryBus` events.

Verify whether `DeliveryBus.doorbell_rang` has an existing player-facing audio or presentation hook. If not, add the smallest safe hook, or file an explicit PM follow-up while still proving a readable in-game doorbell event in the trace.
On unanswered path, preserve outside box and retrieval affordance behavior.
Evidence must show Grete visibly entering/performing the door-answer or carrying intent, not merely that food appears later.
Ensure ordinary `Eat Meal` can consume the delivered food; no special matlevering hunger shortcut.
Run focused filters for visible sequence, matlevering, visitor/courier, held-object, urgent activity, and director.
Capture normal-scene screenshot/video/shot-runner/playthrough trace showing courier and held crate, or file a PM follow-up if tooling blocks capture.
tasks.verification: Integrated sequence passes through production record/director path and has visible evidence. It proves courier + doorbell event/sound/presentation hook or explicit follow-up + Grete visible intent switch + held crate + kitchen food + ordinary eating, not only signal logs.
blockedBy: Task 1: Prove courier visibility through the real visitor path
Task 2: Add a minimal held-crate presentation affordance
Task 7: Extract `MatleveringDeliveryHandler` and migrate Scenario A/B/C behavior

tasks.title: Task 9: Cleanup, docs, full verification, and follow-ups
tasks.description: Shrink temporary scaffolding before final proof, document the new ownership language, run the full suite, and file follow-ups for any explicit deferrals instead of hiding them in SDD-089.
steps: Shrink or delete `MatleveringSimTick` if safe; otherwise leave it as a tiny compatibility façade with comments naming the production owner.
Remove dead helper paths and debug-only delivery/visitor code introduced during migration.

Update `CONTEXT.md` or the appropriate repo doc with enacted Tiltak record, `TiltakRuntimeDirector`, delivery handler, urgent activity seam, held-object affordance, and delivery affordance vocabulary.

File PM follow-ups for explicit deferrals: full standing-tiltak scheduler, robust identity cleanup, richer courier/NPC presentation, doorbell audio/presentation polish if not completed in Task 8, generalized held-object affordances, screenshot tooling.
Run `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter matlevering`, `./tests/run_tests.sh --verbose -- --filter tiltak_runtime_director`, and any separate urgent/visitor/held filters.
Run full `./tests/run_tests.sh`.
Record command outputs and visual artifact paths in PM progress notes before marking final task done.
tasks.verification: Focused filters and full suite pass. Docs name the new boundaries. Follow-ups exist for deferred work. Final report includes exact command output and visible evidence paths.
blockedBy: Task 8: Integrate visible matlevering sequence end-to-end
